role: Senior BI / Analytics Engineer – Strategic Accounts

evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: data quality, security and lineage. Maintain data-quality monitoring, row-level security, GDPR controls
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Multiple rigour signals emphasize governance, security, and quality over delivery speed, anchored in GDPR and audit compliance requirements.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: High domain risk due to explicit financial reporting and regulatory language (GDPR, row-level security, audit lineage for CMR/MBR).
evidence.collaboration_width: Strategic Account Manager; EMEA Strategic Account Manager; clusters
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Established data function with named BI tools, complex dimensional modeling, and specialized SAM team structure implies scale and specialization.
evidence.jd_authorship: Data sourcing and integration. Build pipelines from cluster CRM, ERP, order-billing and pricing systems
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Precise technical context naming specific systems (CRM, ERP, order-billing) and requiring entity resolution and dimensional modeling expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: financial reporting, FP&A, P&L, revenue, price realisation
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary audience is finance/commercial: revenue, resupply metrics, gap-to-plan analysis, price realization tracking.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own ongoing monitoring, refresh, change requests, new-account onboarding and hierarchy updates
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic ownership framed as 'own' with end-to-end accountability for build and run, independent from Leadership specification.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools or AI-ready infrastructure expected from the candidate.
evidence.testing_framing: Data quality, security and lineage. Maintain data-quality monitoring
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing/quality is framed as owned responsibility with explicit accountability for monitoring and governance.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Data quality, security and lineage so numbers hold up in the CMR and MBR
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High loss aversion due to regulatory compliance (GDPR, audit lineage) and risk of bad data reaching financial decision-makers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Build and run the data foundation, dashboards and IBP data feed for the Top 10 EMEA Strategic Accounts
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Fix/scale: existing SAM function, task is to build out and scale the data layer for established account structure.
evidence.loss_aversion: Numbers hold up in the CMR and MBR; maintain data-quality monitoring; GDPR controls — the role is fundamentally about preventing bad data from reaching financial decision-makers and regulators.
